meaning: n.野蛮,粗野,残酷,好战,致命性
examples: "What do you want?" she asked with her usual truculence.
The whole history of their movement is one of intransigence and truculence.
It has the characteristics such as antagonism, truculence, admiring and folk.
As a leading exporter of fossil fuels, Russia may well play a stabilizing role in global energy markets, despite its purported truculence in recent years.
Public hostility to free trade has risen, and has been matched by growing political truculence, notes the report of a task force of trade experts organised by the Council on Foreign Relations.
